title: lappula calycosa
intro: Lappula calycosa, also known as the prickly gilia or Russian knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. Its health benefits include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, as well as alleviating respiratory problems, such as asthma and bronchitis. The herbal preparations of Lappula calycosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
